song.title: When I Survey the Wondrous Cross
song.author: Isaac Watts
song.lyrics:
[V1] 1) When I survey the wondrous cross, On which the Prince of glory died, My richest gain I count but loss, And pour contempt on all my pride.
[V2] 2) Forbid it, Lord that I should boast, Save in the death of Christ my God! All the vain things that charm me most, I sacrifice them to His blood.
[V3] 3) See from His head, His hands, His feet, Sorrow and love flow mingled down! Did e'er such love and sorrow meet, Or thorns compose so rich a crown?
[V4] 4) His dying crimson, like a robe, Spreads o'er His body on the tree; Then I am dead to all the globe, And all the globe is dead to me.
[V5] 5) Were the whole realm of nature mine, That were an off'ring far too small; Love so amazing, so divine, Demands my soul, my life, my all.
[E] 1) When I survey the wondrous cross, On which the Prince of glory died, My richest gain I count but loss, And pour contempt on all my pride.
2) Forbid it, Lord that I should boast, Save in the death of Christ my God! All the vain things that charm me most, I sacrifice them to His blood.
3) See from His head, His hands, His feet, Sorrow and love flow mingled down! Did e'er such love and sorrow meet, Or thorns compose so rich a crown?
4) His dying crimson, like a robe, Spreads o'er His body on the tree; Then I am dead to all the globe, And all the globe is dead to me.
5) Were the whole realm of nature mine, That were an off'ring far too small; Love so amazing, so divine, Demands my soul, my life, my all.
